summary: Built and installed Owlory on the connected physical iPhone, but Tools/generate-build-info.sh stamped GitCommit=no-git into the bundle because the script's `[ -d "$GIT_ROOT/.git" ]` check rejects git worktrees (where .git is a file pointer, not a directory). The on-device Build Info screen would therefore show no-git/no-git, breaking the source-traceability that device-verified proof requires. Did not exercise the Write -> Turn into Task -> View source note flow on device, because completing the flow without traceable build provenance would not have reached device-verified proof; lower proof levels were already achieved by the screenshot-verified slice.
validations_failed: Tools/generate-build-info.sh stamping for the worktree checkout: stamped GitCommit=no-git, GitCommitFull=no-git, GitBranch=no-git, GitTag=no-git instead of the actual commit a849fb9 / branch main / worktree claude/frosty-greider-e0a33c.
recommended_next_reason: Fix the worktree-incompatible git detection in Tools/generate-build-info.sh so on-device builds carry traceable provenance. Without that, device-verified proof cannot be claimed honestly from a worktree checkout. After that lands, retry write-promotion-device-verification.

contract_status_changes.contract: Device-verified proof for Write to Home task source-note return flow
contract_status_changes.before: Slice was queued with depends_on=[owlory-running-app-smoke, write-promotion-screenshot-proof]; both dependencies were already done.
contract_status_changes.after: Slice is blocked because the build-info stamp script does not recognize git worktrees and produces no-git provenance; the installed app cannot be tied to commit a849fb9 via the in-app Build Info screen, so device-verified proof is unreachable from this checkout.
proof: ls -la .git (.git is a file containing 'gitdir: ...' rather than a directory in this worktree)
Tools/generate-build-info.sh:36 (`if [ -n "$GIT_ROOT" ] && [ -d "$GIT_ROOT/.git" ]; then`) — the [ -d ] test fails on worktrees and the script falls through to the no-git fallback at line 47-54.

residual_risks: Tools/generate-build-info.sh uses `[ -d "$GIT_ROOT/.git" ]`, which fails in any git worktree because .git is a file pointer instead of a directory. Any worktree-built install carries no-git provenance and cannot reach device-verified or testflight-verified proof. The fix is small (use `[ -e "$GIT_ROOT/.git" ]` or `git -C "$GIT_ROOT" rev-parse --git-dir` for the existence check) but lives outside this slice's allowed_paths.

The Owlory app is currently installed on the connected device with no-git provenance. It can still be launched and exercised, but the in-app Build Info screen will display no-git/no-git rather than the real commit. Treat any flow run from this install as untraceable until reinstalled from a stamping-fixed build.

BuildInfo.isReleaseable is false for this build (gitCommit is empty/unknown after the no-git fallback), which is correct safety behavior but means the build itself is not a releasable artifact even if the flow worked.

Did not exercise the Write -> Turn into Task -> View source note flow on device this run; running it without traceable provenance would not have raised proof_level beyond what write-promotion-screenshot-proof already established.
